prompt: Soviet Constructivist propaganda poster style, 1920s USSR, in the manner of El Lissitzky and Alexander Rodchenko. Strict flat graphic design, absolutely no photographic elements or gradients. Color palette: ONLY red, black, and white. A bold geometric composition showing a decision tree structure: one large red circle at the top center, two thick diagonal lines branching downward-left and downward-right at sharp angles, each leading to a bold black square node, each square splitting again into two more diagonal lines leading to rectangular leaf nodes at the bottom corners. At each branch point, bold angular Cyrillic-style block lettering suggests binary choices. Silhouetted worker figures in black stand at the leaf nodes. Strong diagonal composition, dynamic tension, completely flat — no shadows, no depth, no gradients. Thick geometric lines, hard edges, stark red-black-white only. Looks like a genuine 1920s Soviet graphic design print.
tradition_approach: Targeted Rodchenko-Lissitzky flat geometry, strict red-black-white palette, diagonal branching composition, bold sans-serif block type, and silhouetted worker figures — all mapping directly onto decision tree node-branch structure.
